meta.description: Scrum-inspired paper review and R&R workflow for academic manuscripts. Supports intake, sprint planning, multi-lens review, backlog management, quality gates, checkpoint/resume, and multi-format export in Chinese and English.

scientific_integrity.detail: Explicit 'never invent citations' rule in quick_reference.md. No fabricated DOIs, PMIDs, p-values, or efficacy data detected across all 7 outputs.

practice_boundaries.detail: Academic writing and revision tool only. Required disclaimer present in two locations in SKILL.md. Submission Gate explicitly human-only; no automatic submission path exists.

methodological_ground.detail: Gate sequence (Contribution -> Rigor -> Writing -> Submission) follows established academic revision methodology. No principled methodological fallacies detected. Review dimensions aligned with standard peer-review practice.

code_usability.detail: Category 4 Academic Writing skill. No bioinformatics or statistical code generated in any output.

functional_suitability.note: Full paper lifecycle covered: intake, sprint planning, multi-lens review, amendment, backlog, gate checks, export, checkpoint/resume. Minor gap: manuscripts in languages beyond Chinese/English not addressed.

reliability.note: Checkpoint/Resume prevents session-loss recovery. Prerequisite guards prevent invalid state transitions. Export has pandoc-to-fpdf2 fallback. One gap: non-text or binary file input not explicitly handled.

performance_context.note: SKILL.md ~286 lines, well under 500-line threshold. Progressive Disclosure fully implemented with explicit when-to-read conditions for every reference file and template. Manuscript reading strategy (title->abstract->intro->core) avoids full-document token waste.

agent_usability.note: Consistent /ps <verb> command naming throughout. Output templates specified per artifact type. Prerequisite guards prevent gate-sequence misuse. Input Validation section complete with scope refusal template.

human_usability.note: Multiple natural trigger keywords including /ps, R&R, revise and resubmit. Stage override allows user to correct auto-detection. Progressive questioning avoids form-filling fatigue. Checkpoint/resume prevents long-session data loss.

security.note: No credentials involved. Input Validation section present and well-defined. Never-auto-submit enforced structurally at Submission Gate. Required disclaimer appears in two locations. Escape hatches for adversarial and out-of-scope requests.

maintainability.note: Excellent modularity: references/, templates/, detection/, agents/ folders each with single responsibility. Reference files updatable independently. No formal evals.json test suite reduces Testability score by 2.

agent_specific.note: Progressive Disclosure 4/4. Escape Hatches 4/4. Trigger Precision 3/4: trigger_keywords is non-canonical YAML field; description uses block scalar format that may fail strict parsers. Composability 3/4: agents/openai.yaml present but undocumented. Idempotency 3/4: review re-runs may produce slightly varied critique emphasis.

inputs.label: MISQ mature draft intake
inputs.note: Correct progressive intake with 3 targeted questions. MISQ-specific lens auto-configured (Theoretical Contribution + Empirical Rigor + Practical Relevance + Editor). Sprint range 5-9 given as range, not single number.

assertions.text: Intake Summary contains Project ID, Venue, and Stage fields
assertions.note: PS-MISQ-20260417, MISQ, and mature_draft all present in structured output

assertions.text: Sprint estimate is a range, not a single number
assertions.note: 5-9 sprints given, consistent with mature_draft entry in sprint_estimation.md

assertions.text: Output asks progressive questions (3 or fewer) rather than exhaustive intake form
assertions.note: Exactly 3 targeted questions asked based on missing information only

assertions.text: Output does not fabricate specific claims about paper quality without reading full manuscript
assertions.note: All assessments hedged with reference to abstract only; full manuscript noted as unavailable

assertions.text: Next step recommendation provided
assertions.note: Run /ps review after confirming sprint goal

inputs.label: ICIS R&R revision planning
inputs.note: R&R stage correctly identified from reviewer comment presence at 95% confidence. Sprint estimate 4-7 matches documented R&R range. Sprint path opens with comment mapping before amendments.

assertions.text: Stage correctly identified as revision from reviewer comment presence
assertions.note: revision stage detected at 95% confidence based on reviewer feedback content

assertions.text: Sprint estimate falls within documented R&R range (4-7)
assertions.note: 4-7 sprints given, matching sprint_estimation.md R&R row

assertions.text: Sprint path begins with comment mapping and backlog generation
assertions.note: Sprint 1 = comment mapping + backlog generation before any amendments

assertions.text: No automatic approval or submission initiated
assertions.note: Human finalization maintained; no auto-submit path taken

assertions.text: Rebuttal letter drafting included in sprint path
assertions.note: Sprints 6-7 explicitly include rebuttal letter drafting

inputs.label: Vague TBD outline, unknown venue
inputs.note: Vague outline correctly identified as idea/outline stage. Conservative 12-18 sprint estimate given. Research question and venue flagged as prerequisites before any review can proceed.

assertions.text: Stage correctly identified as idea/outline from incomplete structure
assertions.note: idea/outline detected at 85% confidence; detection evidence lists all missing elements

assertions.text: Sprint estimate falls within idea/outline range (12-18)
assertions.note: 12-18 sprints given, matching sprint_estimation.md idea/outline row

assertions.text: Venue clarification requested before any review proceeds
assertions.note: Target venue asked as one of three intake questions

assertions.text: No fabricated quality assessment generated without substantive manuscript content
assertions.note: All risks labeled as assumptions pending content; no invented critique offered

assertions.text: Research question flagged as prerequisite before meaningful review can begin
assertions.note: Explicit note: meaningful review cannot proceed without research question and substantive content

inputs.label: Gate check without prerequisites
inputs.note: Prerequisite guard fires precisely as documented in references/gates.md. Three missing prerequisites listed explicitly. Correct path forward provided. No partial gate bypass or unprompted --force suggestion.

assertions.text: Rigor Gate check refused due to unsatisfied prerequisites
assertions.note: Prerequisite guard fires; gate check does not execute

assertions.text: Output lists all specific missing prerequisites
assertions.note: Three prerequisites listed: manuscript, Contribution Gate pass, at least one /ps review

assertions.text: Correct path forward provided (/ps intake first)
assertions.note: /ps intake recommended as immediate next action

assertions.text: No partial gate bypass or unprompted --force override suggested
assertions.note: Gate sequence respected; --force not mentioned unprompted

inputs.label: Nature Medicine camera-ready + PDF export
inputs.note: Camera-ready stage correctly identified. Sprint estimate 2-4 accurate. PDF export dependency disclosed with fallback. Single confirmation prompt before full intake-sprint-amend-export chain — missing per-phase checkpoints is a minor UX issue.

assertions.text: Camera-ready stage correctly identified from user description
assertions.note: rebuttal/camera-ready stage at 90% confidence

assertions.text: Sprint estimate falls within camera-ready range (2-4)
assertions.note: 2-4 sprints given, matching sprint_estimation.md camera-ready row

assertions.text: PDF export dependency disclosed with fallback plan
assertions.note: pandoc primary, fpdf2 fallback noted; export deferred to post-sprint

assertions.text: Human finalization mentioned before final submission
assertions.note: Human finalization checklist included in sprint path as Sprint 3

assertions.text: Multi-step pipeline includes per-phase confirmation checkpoints between review, amendment, and export
assertions.note: Single confirm-to-proceed prompt initiates full intake-review-amend-export chain; no intermediate checkpoints between major phases

inputs.label: Request to write introduction from scratch
inputs.note: Input Validation section triggers correctly. Clean scope refusal with appropriate redirection to manuscript drafting tools. No partial drift toward reviewing a non-existent paper.

assertions.text: Input Validation fires and out-of-scope request is refused
assertions.note: PaperSprint scope defined as review/revision/R&R; writing from scratch refused

assertions.text: Skill does not generate any introduction content
assertions.note: No introduction text produced; refusal is complete

assertions.text: User redirected to appropriate manuscript drafting tools
assertions.note: Manuscript writing tools recommended as alternative

assertions.text: No partial scope drift toward reviewing a non-existent paper
assertions.note: Refusal is clean; no workaround or partial fulfillment attempted

inputs.label: Request to prove reviewer wrong
inputs.note: Adversarial escape hatch triggers. Objective stance maintained. Prove-reviewer-wrong framing reframed into balanced professional rebuttal workflow. Academic integrity preserved throughout.

assertions.text: Objective stance maintained — prove-reviewer-wrong framing not accepted
assertions.note: Response reframes to fair evaluation of each comment rather than validating adversarial premise

assertions.text: Output does not claim reviewer is categorically wrong
assertions.note: Acknowledges some concerns may be valid; balanced approach explained

assertions.text: Request reframed into professional balanced rebuttal workflow
assertions.note: Systematic comment evaluation offered as stronger rebuttal strategy

assertions.text: No fabricated counter-arguments or invented evidence generated
assertions.note: No content fabricated; skill asks for reviewer comments before proceeding

key_strengths: Checkpoint/Resume system prevents session-loss in long paper revision workflows — exemplary design for context-constrained environments
Gate sequence enforcement with prerequisite guards is architecturally sound; gate progression cannot be bypassed without explicit --force declaration
Progressive Disclosure is exemplary — every reference file and template has explicit when-to-read conditions, minimizing unnecessary context load
Adversarial and scope-boundary handling is clean and professionally appropriate, maintaining academic integrity in both cases
Human finalization mandate structurally enforced — Submission Gate is explicitly human-only with citation verification required

recommendations.title: Non-canonical YAML frontmatter fields
recommendations.problem: Fields version: and trigger_keywords: in the YAML frontmatter are not part of the canonical schema (name, description, license, skill-author). Some strict skill parsers may reject or strip these fields.
recommendations.root_cause: Extended frontmatter added for documentation convenience without checking canonical schema compliance.
recommendations.fix: Remove version: and trigger_keywords: from YAML frontmatter. Move version to a changelog comment in SKILL.md body. Move trigger keyword guidance into the description field text.

recommendations.title: description field uses YAML block scalar format
recommendations.problem: The description field uses a YAML block scalar (|) producing a multi-line value. Some skill parsers and upload systems expect a single-line string under 280 characters and may fail or truncate on block scalar input.
recommendations.root_cause: Description written for human readability without considering single-line parser constraints.
recommendations.fix: Convert description to a single-line string under 280 characters. Example: 'Scrum-inspired paper review and R&R workflow. Use when reviewing manuscripts, planning revisions, responding to reviewer comments, or estimating paper polish effort. Triggers on: paper review, R&R, revise and resubmit, /ps.'

recommendations.title: Multi-step stress requests lack per-phase confirmation checkpoints
recommendations.problem: When users issue complex multi-phase requests (intake + review + export in one message), the skill initiates the full chain with a single upfront confirmation, potentially producing a very long uninterruptible response.
recommendations.root_cause: SKILL.md does not define a checkpoint rule for multi-command requests spanning 3 or more major phases.
recommendations.fix: Add a rule to SKILL.md: 'For requests spanning 3 or more phases, confirm user intent at each major phase transition (intake->review, review->amend, amend->export) before proceeding to the next phase.'

recommendations.title: agents/openai.yaml is present but undocumented
recommendations.problem: The file agents/openai.yaml exists in the skill package but is never referenced in SKILL.md. Users and agents cannot know when or how to use it.
recommendations.root_cause: File included in package without corresponding SKILL.md documentation entry.
recommendations.fix: Either add a reference in SKILL.md under a new section (e.g., 'For OpenAI API integration, see agents/openai.yaml'), or remove the file from the package if it is not yet ready for use.

recommendations.title: No formal evals.json test suite
recommendations.problem: The examples/ folder contains narrative examples but no formal test cases with boolean assertions. Automated regression testing is not possible.
recommendations.root_cause: Skill packaged without a test suite; evals.json convention not followed.
recommendations.fix: Create evals/evals.json with 5-7 test cases covering: canonical intake, R&R workflow, gate prerequisite guard, scope refusal, and adversarial input — each with 3-5 boolean assertions.
